methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Renaissance Fusion, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Francesco Volpe and Martin Kupp, has raised $60.5M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 8 private and 3 public comparables, including peers such as Fuse Energy, TerraPower, and Kairos Power, as well as public names OKLO, NNE, and SMR.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $121M valuation. This method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $60.5M total capital raised as a valuation proxy, producing an implied post-money value of $121M. The peer-median approach cannot be applied; while 8 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filter set (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a median calculation unreliable. An all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The triangulated valuation range is $103M to $139M, with a median of $121M. This analysis rests on a single method given insufficient comparable depth in the nuclear energy subsector at the Series A frontier-tech stage. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the 2.0x step-up multiplier to pre-revenue or early-revenue nuclear technology companies, where development timelines and capital intensity may differ materially from broader venture benchmarks. Additional sensitivity analysis against adjusted peer multiples or discounted-cash-flow scenarios would strengthen conviction if underlying technical and commercial milestones become available.
